evidence_quotes.quote: alter the terms after the fact
patently offensive as determined solely at the discretion of the service-provider
terminating or reducing a service without compensating outstanding value to the customer.
withholding an outstanding balance when (unilaterally) terminating a service.
Disallow services using open-ended formulations for terminating use
contractual agreements with said user-facing service

paragraphs.text: Given the recent reports of payment facilitators using terms of service (tos) containing formulations allowing them to to effectively alter the terms after the fact I have a few suggestions of areas which might bear thinking about: 1) Suggest to regulate service-providers terminating or reducing a service without compensating outstanding value to the customer. Examples: 1a) A person buying a product with a function which is later removed, put behind a paywall or monetized by mandatory advertisements. 1b) A payment processor, banking service, digital store, loyalty card service or other similar entity withholding an outstanding balance when (unilaterally) terminating a service. 2) Disallow services using open-ended formulations for terminating use, e.g. "patently offensive as determined solely at the discretion of the service-provider". Usage limitations should be possible to evaluate when signing up for a service. Examples: 2a) A media platform disallowing political, adult or similar content in an inconsistent or changing manner making it unfeasible to users to determine what is accepted. 3) Shield users from second-hand restrictions by regulating not only end-user agreements, but also looking at what requirements can be imposed on user-facing services by other entities in contractual agreements with said user-facing service.
